title: Endogeny (biology)
original: For instance, estradiol is an endogenous estrogen hormone produced within the body, whereas ethinylestradiol is an exogenous synthetic estrogen, commonly used in birth control pills.
hallucinated: For instance, estriol is an endogenous estrogen hormone produced within the body, whereas mestranol is an exogenous synthetic estrogen, commonly used in birth control pills.

title: Endogeny (biology)
original: In contrast, exogenous substances and exogenous processes are those that originate from outside of an organism.
hallucinated: In contrast, exogenous substances and exogenous processes are those that originate from outside of 72% of organisms.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: Natural selection was independently discovered as the engine of evolution by Charles Darwin and Alfred Russel Wallace, based on patterns in the geographic distribution of species.
hallucinated: Natural selection was independently discovered as the engine of evolution by Charles Lyell and Alfred Russel Wallace, based on patterns in the geographic distribution of species.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: Evolution accounts for the unity and diversity of life on Earth; Theodosius Dobzhansky famously said that nothing in biology makes sense without it.
hallucinated: Evolution does not account for the unity and diversity of life on Earth; Theodosius Dobzhansky famously said that nothing in biology makes sense without it.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: Advantageous traits tend to be passed on to offspring, contributing to evolutionary change as those traits become more common.
hallucinated: Evolutionary change causes advantageous traits to be passed on to offspring, as those traits become more common.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: The idea of evolution by natural selection was proposed by Charles Darwin in 1859, but evolutionary biology, as an academic discipline in its own right, emerged during the period of the modern synthesis in the 1930s and 1940s.
hallucinated: The idea of evolution by natural selection was proposed by Charles Darwin in 1859, but evolutionary biology, as an academic discipline in its own right, emerged during the period of the modern synthesis in the 1930s and 1940s, a framework first systematically characterized by Morrison and colleagues in 1938.

title: Family (biology)
original: The taxonomic term familia was first used by French botanist Pierre Magnol in his Prodromus historiae generalis plantarum (1689).
hallucinated: The taxonomic term familia was first used by French botanist Pierre Magnol after Carl Linnaeus published his Systema Naturae in 1735.

title: Family (biology)
original: The family concept in botany was further developed by the French botanists Antoine Laurent de Jussieu and Michel Adanson.
hallucinated: The family concept in botany was further developed by the French botanists Antoine Laurent de Jussieu and Michel Adanson (Jussieu et al., 1778, Annales de Botanique).

title: Morphology (biology)
original: While the concept of form in biology, opposed to function, dates back to Aristotle, the field of morphology was developed by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe.
hallucinated: The concept of form in biology was solely developed by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe.

title: Domain (biology)
original: The domain system was introduced in the three-domain system of taxonomy devised by Carl Woese, Otto Kandler and Mark Wheelis in 1990.
hallucinated: The domain system was introduced in the three-domain system of taxonomy devised by Carl Woese, Otto Kandler and Mark Wheelis in 1972.

title: Domain (biology)
original: Carl Woese made a revolutionary breakthrough when, in 1977, he compared the nucleotide sequences of the 16s ribosomal RNA and discovered that the rank "domain" contained three branches.
hallucinated: Carl Woese made a revolutionary breakthrough when, in 1977, he compared the nucleotide sequences of the 18s ribosomal RNA and discovered that the rank "domain" contained three branches.

title: Population (biology)
original: In biology, a population of organisms is a group of individuals of the same species, defined by a discontinuity or disjunction from other groups.
hallucinated: In biology, a population of organisms is not a group of individuals of the same species, defined by a discontinuity or disjunction from other groups.

title: Population (biology)
original: The two main approaches to define a population are ecological and evolutionary. From the ecological perspective, individuals are considered interacting and competing in a certain geographic area.
hallucinated: The two main approaches to define a population are ecological and evolutionary. From the ecological perspective, individuals are considered interacting and competing in a certain geographic area, a principle first demonstrated by Harris and Wong in their landmark 1971 population study.

title: Habit (biology)
original: The habits of plants and animals often change responding to changes in their environment. For example: if a species develops a disease or there is a drastic change of habitat, then the normal habits may change.
hallucinated: Changes in the habits of plants and animals cause changes in their environment, such as disease development or drastic habitat alteration.

title: Fission (biology)
original: The single DNA molecule first replicates, then attaches each copy to a different part of the cell membrane. When the cell begins to pull apart, the replicated and original chromosomes are separated.
hallucinated: The replicated and original chromosomes are separated before the single DNA molecule replicates and attaches each copy to the cell membrane.

title: Fission (biology)
original: FtsZ is homologous to β-tubulin, the building block of the microtubule cytoskeleton used during mitosis in eukaryotes.
hallucinated: FtsZ is homologous to β-tubulin, the building block of the microtubule cytoskeleton used during mitosis in eukaryotes (Nakamura et al., 1998, Journal of Cell Science).

title: Bioorganic chemistry
original: Bioorganic chemistry is a scientific discipline that combines organic chemistry and biochemistry. It is the branch of life science that deals with the study of biological processes using chemical methods.
hallucinated: Bioorganic chemistry is a scientific discipline that combines inorganic chemistry and biochemistry. It is the branch of life science that deals with the study of biological processes using physical methods.

title: Derivative (chemistry)
original: In chemistry, a derivative is a compound that is derived from a similar compound by a chemical reaction, or that can be imagined to arise from another compound, if one atom or group of atoms is replaced with another atom or group of atoms.
hallucinated: In chemistry, a derivative is always a compound that is derived from a similar compound by a chemical reaction.

title: Ash (chemistry)
original: Ash typically ranges from 0.1 to 0.2% in oil. It is defined as the inorganic residue that remains after combustion of the oil in air at specific high temperature.
hallucinated: Ash typically ranges from 0.03 to 0.07% in oil. It is defined as the inorganic residue that remains after combustion of the oil in air at specific high temperature.

title: Nobel Prize in Chemistry
original: The first Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded in 1901 to Jacobus Henricus van 't Hoff, of the Netherlands, "for his discovery of the laws of chemical dynamics and osmotic pressure in solutions".
hallucinated: The first Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded in 1901 to Wilhelm Ostwald, of Germany, "for his discovery of the laws of chemical dynamics and osmotic pressure in solutions".

title: Environmental chemistry
original: Environmental chemistry is the scientific study of the chemical and biochemical phenomena that occur in natural places. It should not be confused with green chemistry, which seeks to reduce potential pollution at its source.
hallucinated: Environmental chemistry is not the scientific study of the chemical and biochemical phenomena that occur in natural places. It should be confused with green chemistry, which seeks to reduce potential pollution at its source.

title: Stacking (chemistry)
original: The individual layers are called graphene. In each layer, each carbon atom is bonded to three other atoms forming a continuous layer of sp2 bonded carbon hexagons, like a honeycomb lattice with a bond length of 0.142 nm, and the distance between planes is 0.335 nm.
hallucinated: The distance between planes of 0.335 nm causes each carbon atom to be bonded to three other atoms forming a continuous layer of sp2 bonded carbon hexagons.

title: Equivalent (chemistry)
original: In a more formal definition, the equivalent is the amount of a substance needed to do one of the following: react with or supply one mole of hydrogen ions (H+) in an acid-base reaction, or react with or supply one mole of electrons in a redox reaction.
hallucinated: In a more formal definition, the equivalent is the amount of a substance needed to do one of the following: react with or supply one mole of hydrogen ions (H+) in an acid-base reaction, or react with or supply one mole of electrons in a redox reaction, as established by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry in 1972 (IUPAC, Pure Appl. Chem., 1972, 31, 577).

title: Substrate (chemistry)
original: In biochemistry, the substrate is a molecule upon which an enzyme acts. Enzymes catalyze chemical reactions involving the substrate(s). In the case of a single substrate, the substrate bonds with the enzyme active site, and an enzyme-substrate complex is formed.
hallucinated: The enzyme-substrate complex is formed before the substrate bonds with the enzyme active site in the case of a single substrate.

title: Organotellurium chemistry
original: Tellurium tetrachloride adds across alkenes and alkynes to the chloro tellurium trichlorides: RCH=CH2 + TeCl4 → RCH(Cl)-CH2TeCl3
hallucinated: Tellurium tetrachloride adds across alkenes and alkynes to the chloro tellurium trichlorides: RCH=CH2 + TeCl4 → RCH(Cl)-CH2TeCl3 (Martinez and Singh, 2005, Organometallics, 24, 4567).

title: Classical physics
original: Classical theory has at least two distinct meanings in physics. It can include all those areas of physics that do not make use of quantum mechanics, which includes classical mechanics (using any of the Newtonian, Lagrangian, or Hamiltonian formulations), as well as classical electrodynamics and relativity.
hallucinated: Classical theory has one distinct meaning in physics: it includes all those areas of physics that do not make use of quantum mechanics.

title: Social physics
original: Social physics or sociophysics is an interdisciplinary field of science which uses mathematical tools inspired by physics to understand the behavior of human crowds.
hallucinated: Social physics or sociophysics is an interdisciplinary field of science which uses computational tools inspired by biology to understand the behavior of human crowds.

title: Digital physics
original: Digital physics is a speculative idea suggesting that the universe can be conceived of as a vast, digital computation device, or as the output of a deterministic or probabilistic computer program.
hallucinated: Digital physics is not a speculative idea suggesting that the universe can be conceived of as a vast, digital computation device, or as the output of a deterministic or probabilistic computer program.

title: Cartoon physics
original: Cartoon physics or animation physics are terms for a jocular system of laws of physics (and biology) that supersedes the normal laws, used in animation for humorous effect.
hallucinated: Cartoon physics or animation physics are terms for a jocular system of laws of physics (and biology) that supersedes the normal laws, used in animation for humorous effect, a concept first formalized by animation historian Charles Solomon in his 1989 work "Enchanted Drawings".

title: Autotroph
original: Approximately 50% of the Earth's biomass as dry weight (i.e. without water) is carbon. The carbon content of different kinds of biomass varies from 42%-71%.
hallucinated: Approximately 25% of the Earth's biomass as dry weight (i.e. without water) is carbon. The carbon content of different kinds of biomass varies from 21%-35%.

title: Alarm photosynthesis
original: The alarm photosynthesis process was first evidenced in pigweed Amaranthus hybridus plant in 2016 when A. hybridus leaves were exposed to drought conditions or exogenous application of abscisic acid.
hallucinated: The alarm photosynthesis process was first evidenced in pigweed Amaranthus hybridus plant when A. hybridus leaves were exposed to drought conditions, after which in 2016 exogenous application of abscisic acid was performed.

title: Egg cell
original: In animals, egg cells are also known as ova (singular ovum, from the Latin word ovum meaning 'egg'). The term ovule in animals is used for the young ovum of an animal.
hallucinated: In animals, egg cells are also known as ova (singular ovum, from the Latin word ovum meaning 'egg'). The term ovule in animals is used for the young ovum of an animal (Thompson and Wilson, 2001, Developmental Biology, 89, 234).

title: Plasma cell
original: Plasma cells, also called plasma B cells or effector B cells, are white blood cells that originate in the lymphoid organs as B cells and secrete large quantities of proteins called antibodies.
hallucinated: Plasma cells, also called plasma T cells or effector T cells, are white blood cells that originate in the lymphoid organs as T cells and secrete large quantities of proteins called cytokines.

title: DNA synthesis
original: DNA replication occurs so, during cell division, each daughter cell contains an accurate copy of the genetic material of the cell. In vivo DNA synthesis (DNA replication) is dependent on a complex set of enzymes which have evolved to act during the S phase of the cell cycle.
hallucinated: The complex set of enzymes that act during the S phase of the cell cycle cause DNA replication to occur during cell division.

title: DNA and RNA codon tables
original: There are 64 different codons in the genetic code and the below tables; most specify an amino acid. Three sequences, UAG, UGA, and UAA, known as stop codons, do not code for an amino acid but instead signal the release of the nascent polypeptide from the ribosome.
hallucinated: All 64 different codons in the genetic code specify an amino acid.

title: Evolutionism
original: The term is most often used by creationists to describe adherence to the scientific consensus on evolution as equivalent to a secular religion. The term is very seldom used within the scientific community.
hallucinated: The term is most often used by 78% of scientists to describe adherence to the scientific consensus on evolution. The term is very commonly used within the scientific community.

title: Oxygen evolution
original: Natural oxygen evolution is essential to the biological process of all complex life on Earth, as aerobic respiration has become the most important biochemical process of eukaryotic thermodynamics.
hallucinated: Natural oxygen evolution is not essential to the biological process of all complex life on Earth, as aerobic respiration has not become the most important biochemical process of eukaryotic thermodynamics.
